_meta.purpose: Lossless handoff for the ACL Outcomes study landing page. Contains all content, design, architecture, and deployment information needed to recreate, update, or adapt the page in a new Claude chat session.
_meta.opening_instruction: Load this JSON first. It contains everything needed to update or rebuild the ACL Outcomes landing page at https://andrewhallorthosports.github.io/acl-outcomes/. Read all sections before making any changes. Use the github_deployment section for all pushes. Follow standing_rules without exception.

project_identity.study_name: Clinical, Functional and Sport-Related Outcomes Following ACL Reconstruction
project_identity.presentation_note: Conference reference removed from live page per standing rules — appears only in footer citation and presentation section
project_identity.institution_primary: STRIKERS Elite Sports Group, Dublin, Ireland
project_identity.institution_secondary: School of Medicine, University of St Andrews, UK
project_identity.hospital_note: Always 'Royal Infirmary of Edinburgh' — never 'Royal Infirmary Edinburgh'
project_identity.survey_note: No associated survey for this project
project_identity.ga_note: Shared across all STRIKERS pages — same ID

key_findings.risk_factor_finding: After adjustment for confounders, no independent variable was associated with reoperation (p ≥ 0.082) or revision ACL reconstruction (p ≥ 0.161)
conclusions: 1 in 6 underwent further surgery within 2 years; revision ACLr (6.3%), arthrolysis (4.9%), meniscectomy (3.5%).
Risk of reoperation and revision was not predicted by age, sex, mechanism of injury, or index procedure.
Satisfaction and function were high, yet the impact on work, relationships, and return to sport was substantial.
These findings support honest informed consent and high-quality surgical and rehabilitation support.

hero_figure.description: X-ray style knee anatomy photo — blue wireframe knee with orange/red ACL highlighted centrally
hero_figure.treatment: Circular crop via border-radius:50%, blue glow via filter:drop-shadow(0 0 40px rgba(74,144,217,0.4)), figFloat animation

page_architecture.type: Single self-contained HTML — all assets base64 embedded

sections_in_order.left: Combined STRIKERS + St Andrews logo (30px height)
sections_in_order.right: Section links (Results, PROMs, Conclusions, Slides) + '← All Research' button linking to hub
sections_in_order.hub_btn_style: font-family JetBrains Mono, 9px, OA purple colour, 1px border, border-radius 3px
sections_in_order.mobile: section links hidden below 640px, hub button always visible

sections_in_order.layout: 2-column grid on desktop (text left, figure right), single column on mobile
text_elements: hero-eyebrow: 'STRIKERS Elite Sports Group · School of Medicine, University of St Andrews'
hero-tag: removed (was IOC/UKCCIIS — removed per standing rules)
hero-title: 'Clinical, Functional and Sport-Related / Outcomes After ACL Reconstruction' with span in #7AB8E8
hero-sub: 512 consecutive patients, minimum 24-month follow-up, Royal Infirmary of Edinburgh
hero-authors: REMOVED — authors now in authors-strip below partner banner
sections_in_order.figure: Knee anatomy photo — circular, blue glow, figFloat animation

sections_in_order.institutional_sub: EKAD Project Group: I Daniels · F Dyer · K Milne · J M Leow · S Silvestre · G Snowden · R Webb · Royal Infirmary of Edinburgh · School of Medicine, University of St Andrews

sections.name: Further Surgery
sections.title: Approximately one in six needed further surgery
sections.lead: 90 of 512 patients (17.6%) underwent further ipsilateral knee surgery. Mean time to further surgery was 15.5 months (range 2–55 months).

sections.name: Risk Factors
sections.title: No association with age, sex, mechanism, or procedure

sections_in_order.title: High satisfaction and good function — but a substantial wider burden
sections_in_order.lead: 268 of 512 patients completed the survey (52%). Mean follow-up at time of PROMs collection: 47 months.
components: Return to play subsection (52wks median, 31% did not return within 4yr)
Top sports pill tags (Football 135, Golf 92, Gym 71, Running 50, Rugby 47)

sections_in_order.title: What this means for patients and clinicians
Share or get in touch
components: QR code block (points to acl-outcomes page URL)

sections_in_order.text: STRIKERS Elite Sports Group, Dublin, Ireland · School of Medicine, University of St Andrews, UK / Cullinan et al. 2026 / ajh52@st-andrews.ac.uk

css_components.stats_strip: .stats-inner grid 2/3/6 col responsive. .stat-n JetBrains Mono 28px 700 white. .stat-l 8px 400 rgba(255,255,255,0.45)
css_components.surgery_table: thead bg:#1A3F6F white. tbody hover:var(--acl-pale). td last-child font-weight:600 text-align:right
css_components.slide_drawer: bg:#0A0A14. max-height:75vh overflow-y:auto. .s-pgn pale bg + border-bottom
css_components.authors_strip: pale bg, border-bottom. .ci font-weight:600 color:var(--ink). .ci-role 8px light. .authors-sub 8px light dashed-top-border

js_functions.doShare: navigator.share() on mobile, navigator.clipboard.writeText() on desktop, shows #shareDone for 3s
js_functions.sharePage: doShare('ACL Outcomes — STRIKERS Research','Clinical, functional and sport-related outcomes following ACL reconstruction — analysis of 512 consecutive patients.',PAGE_URL)

github_deployment.ga_snippet: Immediately after <head> tag — async gtag.js + config G-WBBKXNFM9F

push_method.note: File is >1MB — must use git blob API, not contents API
steps: POST /git/blobs with new base64 — get new_blob_sha
push_method.token: Classic PAT repo scope — retrieve from Andrew's secure storage; do not store in handoff

hub_card.figure: Knee anatomy photo circular (same istockphoto asset), 220x220px object-fit:cover border-radius:50%
hub_card.name: ACL Reconstruction Outcomes
hub_card.desc: Clinical, functional, and sport-related outcomes following ACL reconstruction — analysis of 512 consecutive patients at the Royal Infirmary of Edinburgh.

content_to_replace_for_new_study: Hero h1 title and accent span colour
Hero figure — swap knee photo for new study figure
Authors strip — full name list, Lead name, institutional sub-line, project group
All results section content — data, tables, stat cards
Presentation slides base64 data and slide count
Contact section QR base64 and target URL
Email subject line in mailto href
GA Measurement ID (both instances in GA snippet)
GitHub repo name in all URLs
standing_rules: No em dashes — use colons, commas, or parentheses
No conference or event references on live pages — removed from hero, footer, nav, all sections
No bold on A J Hall — (CI) role label only, font-weight:600 via .ci class
First author (R Cullinan) — (Lead) role label only, same .ci class treatment
No <strong> tags on any author name
Hospital: always 'Royal Infirmary of Edinburgh' — never 'Royal Infirmary Edinburgh'
No associated survey for this project — no survey section, no QR pointing to survey
QR code points to the study page URL, not a survey URL
All pages include Other STRIKERS Research nav dropdown (not present on ACL page — add if rebuilding nav)

next_steps_outstanding.prisma_flow: Not applicable — cohort study not scoping review
next_steps_outstanding.other_strikers_dropdown: The ACL page nav does not include the Other STRIKERS Research dropdown present on PM page — consider adding for consistency
next_steps_outstanding.manuscript: Conference presentation complete. Journal submission not yet initiated.
next_steps_outstanding.additional_analyses: Risk factor analysis complete. Further subgroup analyses not yet planned.
